Thought 6: The narrative strongly points to Kimberly as the murderer. She had the opportunity (delivered the drink Jamal consumed immediately), the means (possession of the frog and knowledge of poison extraction), and a deceptive background suggesting criminal intent. Lillian, while having a motive and access, lacks the direct evidence Kimberly has. The detective's focus on Kimberly at the end, including her research and multiple identities, seals the case.
